name: Fairfax VA, USA (Base)

rent.notes: Virginia has no rental tax. Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills (not included in rent).
rent.gigInternetNotes: Fios covers nearly all of Fairfax County with symmetrical gig service. Most single-family rentals in NOVA are Fios-eligible. Minimal filtering required.

groceries.notes: NOVA grocery prices 15-20% above national average

electricity.notes: Dominion Energy. Avg across seasons — $100 winter, $220 summer A/C. Includes general household.

gas.notes: Washington Gas. Natural gas heating (Nov–Mar primary), cooking, water heater.

sewage.notes: Billed with water. Fairfax County sanitary sewer charge.

trash.notes: Fairfax County solid waste. Billed quarterly (~$90/qtr).

utilities.taxes: Virginia exempts residential utilities from state sales tax.
utilities.notes: Electricity (heating+cooling), gas, water, sewage, trash. Internet is in entertainment (Cable+1Gig).

healthcare.notes: Medicare Part B ($174.70/mo) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo x2 people) + Part D (~$45/mo x2) + dental/vision plans. Higher premiums reflect macular degeneration specialist coverage and frequent monitoring needs.

healthcarePreMedicare.notes: ACA silver benchmark for 2-adult household both age ~60, before subsidies. County-level estimate (Fairfax County, VA). NOVA marketplace: Anthem HealthKeepers, CareFirst, Kaiser. Metro pricing. Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

petCare.notes: Vet care in NOVA premium-priced. VCA, Pender, and Fairfax Animal Hospital nearby.

petDaycare.notes: Doggy daycare 2 days/week, large breed rate (~$48/day). Camp Bow Wow, Dogtopia, Whole Dogz in Fairfax area. NoVA premium pricing.

petGrooming.notes: Bernese coat maintenance, professional grooming monthly. NOVA grooming prices above average.

transportation.notes: Car-dependent but Metro Orange/Silver lines accessible from Vienna/Falls Church. Gas, insurance, parking higher in NOVA.

entertainment.notes: Incl cable+1Gig internet, Netflix, Amazon Prime, Apple TV+, dining out (lunch for 2 2x/week w/ tax+tip)

phoneCell.notes: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials. 2 phones, 1.5GB+ high-speed data each, all taxes included.

miscellaneous.notes: HOA contributions, parking permits, EZ-Pass tolls common in NOVA

buffer.notes: Monthly safety buffer for unexpected expenses

medicalOOP.notes: Anti-VEGF injections for macular degeneration (~$300-400 OOP each, 6-10x/yr after Medicare 80%). Specialist visits: ophthalmologist q6-8wks, endocrinologist 2-4x/yr, cardiologist 1-2x/yr. Frequent labs: lipid panel, TSH, A1C, CMP 3-4x/yr each. Both adults: PCP visits quarterly.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

stateIncomeTax.exemptions: Age 65+ deduction $12K/person. SS fully exempt from VA tax.

salesTax.notes: Fairfax County 6% (4.3% state + 0.7% regional + 1% NOVA transit)

propertyTax.notes: Renters: no direct property tax. Vehicle personal property ~$4.57/$100 assessed.

healthcare.notes: Inova Fairfax Medical Center (Level I trauma), Virginia Hospital Center, access to Johns Hopkins/Georgetown specialists. Excellent healthcare infrastructure.

acaMarketplace.notes: NOVA marketplace: Anthem HealthKeepers, CareFirst, Kaiser. Metro pricing.
acaMarketplace.disclaimer: Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

lifestyle.notes: Burke Lake Park, Lake Accotink, numerous dog parks. Diverse dining scene. Close to DC cultural amenities.

pros: Proximity to DC cultural resources (Smithsonian, etc.)
